text:
There was this kid named Oliver who loved collecting seashells. He lived near a beach. One particularly sunny autumn day, Oliver filled three sturdy boxes with shells.

That evening, he took them to a quiet park.

"I hear owls," Oliver whispered.

He looked into an owl's big eye.

"Like this one," he said.

He winked.

Oliver sung owl songs.

That night, an owl answered.

Oliver was delighted. He went home.

Each shell told a story. Each night, an owl listened.

Maybe Owls just like jazz.

They slept under an old red oak.

That fall, Owls came every moon.

They were sweet like honey.

A star owl stayed.

She watched movies.

They flew together.

That's my favorite star owl story.

They'd always wander off.

But never far.

And that's okay.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
